reference: These spots are likely to be small purpuric spots. As the child is otherwise well, this may be idiopathic thrombocytopenic purpura or some clotting factor deficiency. Investigate with a complete blood count, prothrombin time, and activated partial thromboplastin time. There is spontaneous painful bleeding in these conditions.
